vocabulary.definition: A 3D shape with a polygon base and triangular faces that meet at a point (apex)
vocabulary.visual: Looks like an Egyptian pyramid — square base, triangle sides

vocabulary.definition: The height of a triangular face measured along the surface from base to apex
vocabulary.visual: The distance along the slanted side, not straight up

vocabulary.definition: A triangular face on the side of a pyramid (not the base)
vocabulary.visual: The triangle sides that slope up to the top

vocabulary.definition: The bottom face of a pyramid, which is a polygon (square, rectangle, triangle, etc.)
vocabulary.visual: The flat bottom the pyramid sits on

launch.narrative: Your class is building a pyramid-shaped display to showcase the time capsule at the school entrance. The pyramid will be covered in gold leaf to make it shine. To figure out how much gold leaf you need, you must calculate the total surface area — the base plus all the triangular faces!
launch.contextImage: Students assembling a square pyramid display covered in gold leaf for a time capsule exhibit
noticePrompts: How many faces does a square pyramid have?
What shape is the base? What shape are the side faces?
What is the difference between the height of the pyramid and the slant height?
wonderPrompts: Why do we use slant height instead of regular height for the triangular faces?
Would a taller pyramid always need more gold leaf than a shorter one?

explore.instructions: Calculate the surface area of each pyramid display. SA = Base Area + Lateral Area. For each triangular face, use A = ½ × base × slant height.

discourse.prompt: Display B has the largest surface area (280 in²). What contributes more to the total — the base or the lateral faces? Why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: For Display B, the base area is ___ in² and the total lateral area is 4 × ___ = ___ in². The ___ contribute(s) more because ___.

approaching.stem: A square pyramid has a base edge of 5 in and a slant height of 7 in. What is the surface area?
approaching.explanation: Base: 5 × 5 = 25 in². Each lateral face: ½ × 5 × 7 = 17.5 in². Four faces: 4 × 17.5 = 70 in². SA = 25 + 70 = 95 in².

approaching.stem: How many lateral (triangular) faces does a square pyramid have?
approaching.explanation: A square pyramid has a square base and 4 triangular lateral faces — one for each side of the square.

onLevel.instructions: Find the surface area of each square pyramid.

onLevel.stem: A square pyramid has a base area of 49 cm² and a total lateral area of 84 cm². What is the surface area?
onLevel.explanation: SA = Base Area + Lateral Area = 49 + 84 = 133 cm².

extending.title: Find the Surface Area Error

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: Square pyramid: base edge = 10 in, slant height = 8 in

workedExample.label: Lateral Area
workedExample.work: ½ × 10 × 8 = 40 in² (one face). Total lateral = 40 × 3 = 120 in²

extending.correctWork: A square pyramid has 4 lateral faces, not 3. Total lateral = 40 × 4 = 160 in². Correct: SA = 100 + 160 = 260 in².
hints: Check Step 3: how many lateral faces does a square pyramid have?
A square base has 4 sides, so there are 4 triangular faces. The student only multiplied by 3.

connect.scenario: A museum is building a square pyramid display case. The base edge is 3 feet and the slant height is 4 feet. The glass costs $12 per square foot.
connect.promptQuestion: How much will the glass for all 4 triangular sides cost? (The base is open — no glass needed.)
connect.prompt: Each triangular face has area = ½ × ___ × ___ = ___ ft². Four faces: 4 × ___ = ___ ft². Cost: ___ × $12 = $___.

exitTicket.stem: A square pyramid has a base edge of 6 in and a slant height of 5 in. What is the total surface area?
exitTicket.explanation: Base: 6 × 6 = 36 in². Each lateral face: ½ × 6 × 5 = 15 in². Four faces: 4 × 15 = 60 in². SA = 36 + 60 = 96 in². Surface area uses square units (in²).
